articles.title: CAN FINALLY TELL YOU.
articles.summary: Until now, most agencies had no way to know.

articles.title: Client asks, you deliver the data. Free to try for 14 days.
THE B2B BLOG IS COLLAPSING. THE COMPANIES REPLACING IT ARE BECOMING PUBLISHERS
articles.summary: The SEO blog playbook is losing ground as zero-click search rises and platform reach declines. Owned media replaces it, with brands building publications for a defined audience. This model drives distribution through shares, interviews, and syndication. It also turns content into pipeline. Early results show traction from niche audiences and growing AI referrals.

articles.title: WHY SAVVY MEDIA BUYERS ARE BETTING ON MALL ADVERTISING
articles.summary: Media buyers are increasingly turning to mall advertising because it delivers something scarce in a fragmented media landscape: physically present and highly attentive audiences rather than sheer digital reach. Malls have shifted from pure retail destinations into lifestyle hubs, drawing people in for fitness classes and healthcare appointments as often as for shopping. That inventory is now largely digitized, so buyers can target every screen within a set distance of a specific location and measure results through foot traffic, offsite visits, and spend data instead of modeled estimates.

articles.title: FRENCH LAWMAKERS APPROVE A SWEEPING SOCIAL MEDIA BAN FOR CHILDREN UNDER 15
articles.summary: France's ban arrives alongside a broader European push on kids and social media, with the EU's top official calling for limits and an EU advisory panel recommending zero access for children under 13 until platforms can prove their products are safe. Spain and the UK have each floated similar restrictions, and the European Commission is expected to propose EU-wide rules for its 27 member countries.

articles.title: HOW TO BUILD CURIOSITY INTO HIGH-PERFORMING SOCIAL ADS
articles.summary: Attention gets a viewer to notice a social ad, but curiosity is what keeps them from scrolling away in the next few seconds. Platforms are increasingly built to reward the second half of that equation. Meta and TikTok's AI-driven delivery systems now optimize for watch time, saves and shares, and clicks that happen after the video ends. These are all signals a genuinely curious viewer produces without prompting. Building that gap into the creative means designing open loops, such as a surprising statement or an unresolved problem, then demonstrating a result instead of describing it.

articles.title: WHEN AND HOW TO CALL CUSTOMERS BY NAME
articles.summary: Using a customer's name can increase spending and satisfaction when it feels natural and personal. Customers spent 17.3% more on average and accepted upsell offers more than twice as often when employees used their name during the interaction instead of only during the sales pitch. Introduce employees by name first and train staff to use customer names sparingly because scripted or repetitive use reduces the effect. Avoid using customer names in private or sensitive situations because discomfort can reverse the benefit.

articles.title: USE MY /NO-AI-SLOP SKILL TO REMOVE 20+ PATTERNS OF AI SLOP FROM YOUR WRITING
articles.summary: The free /no-ai-slop AI skill removes more than 20 common AI writing patterns from emails, documents, and other content. It can also detect AI-generated writing by identifying those same patterns and listing what it finds. The skill targets habits such as generic openers, fake insights, vague analysis, and other repetitive phrasing that makes AI writing easy to spot. You can install the skill from its GitHub repository and use it in Codex, Claude Code, or other AI coding assistants.

articles.title: AI VISIBILITY IS A TOPIC-LEVEL GAME: A STUDY OF 50,000 BRANDS IN CHATGPT
articles.summary: A study of 50K brands found that winning one ChatGPT prompt does not mean owning a topic. Only 15% of categories had a clear leader, leaving most topics open for competitors. Traditional SEO metrics like traffic and authority scores only predicted winners about half the time. Brands that consistently appear across related questions build stronger AI visibility. Once a brand gains a wide lead, it becomes harder to overtake.

articles.title: HOW TO KNOW YOU'RE READY FOR A FRACTIONAL CMO AND HOW TO BRIEF ONE WELL
articles.summary: Most companies wait until something breaks before hiring a fractional CMO. Readiness isn't about size, it's about having a real strategy gap and a team that can execute. Common signals include weak positioning and looming inflection points like a fundraise or expansion. It's not a fit without product market fit or an execution layer. Bring clear problems, real data, and defined decision rights to get value quickly.

articles.title: MEASUREMENT IS A BUSINESS ADVANTAGE
articles.summary: Better measurement creates a competitive advantage because it uncovers profitable channels, improves ad platform optimization, and helps teams make budget decisions with confidence. Click-based reporting often undervalues channels like podcasts, influencers, and sponsorships, so companies that combine attribution surveys and brand signals can find lower cost opportunities before competitors do. Better conversion data also helps Google and Meta optimize for higher quality customers instead of low value leads.

articles.title: INSIDE GENERAL MILLS'S BRAND ARCHIVE
articles.summary: General Mills's brand archive functions as both a lookup service and a forward-looking curation system, holding more than 10,000 records across 5,000 square feet that trace the company's 160-year history. Brand teams query it for context on products like Bisquick and Betty Crocker, while the three-person team also flags today's campaigns as candidates for tomorrow's collection. The volume and variety of modern marketing content, spanning far more channels than earlier decades, has made deciding what to keep the hardest ongoing call.

articles.title: OUR GOLDEN RULE ON VIDEO VS CAROUSEL
articles.summary: Use video for one takeaway and carousels for multi or technical ideas.

articles.title: THE MOST VALUABLE THING APPLE BUILT IN THE 2000S WASN'T THE IPOD
articles.summary: Apple treated iTunes as a customer acquisition tool because song sales supported iPod adoption and later removed friction for products like the App Store and Apple Pay.
